title: Sweet orange and patchouli room spray
subtitle: A warm citrus-earthy fragrance for living spaces
excerpt: A room spray made with isopropyl alcohol, sweet orange and patchouli essential oils, and distilled water. The bright citrus of sweet orange is anchored by the earthy, persistent patchouli base. A good all-round living-room or hallway fragrance.
sourceNotes: Sweet orange essential oil flash point: approximately 60 to 65°C. Patchouli essential oil flash point: above 90°C. Both above room temperature and above the volatilisation point in this spray format. Room spray formulation: standard IPA-based carrier. Total essential oil at 8% of formula weight for a moderate room throw.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 spray bottle (~100 ml, approximately 60 to 80 sprays)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Total essential oil stays at 8% of formula weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Shake before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store away from heat and sunlight. Keeps 12 months. Sweet orange fades before patchouli; the spray may smell primarily of patchouli after 6 to 9 months. Shake before each use.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which an essential oil can ignite near a flame. Sweet orange has a flash point close to the lower end of the safe range for this spray; patchouli's flash point is above 90°C and provides no safety concern.

content.text: Sweet orange is one of the lightest essential oils: it evaporates quickly, smells bright and immediately pleasant, but does not last long without a heavier base note to anchor it. Patchouli is one of the heaviest: it is slow-releasing and persistent, earthy and slightly sweet. Together the combination provides both the immediate fresh note and the staying power in a room. The

of sweet orange (approximately 60 to 65°C) is above room temperature; the more flammable component in this spray is the isopropyl alcohol carrier. Keep away from naked flames. The spray is made by dissolving the essential oils in the alcohol first (they are soluble in alcohol but not in water), then adding water. Shake before each use as the oils will settle over time.

items.prepNote: 5% of formula; fresh citrus top note
3% of formula; earthy, persistent base note

content.text: Add both essential oils to the spray bottle first. Add the isopropyl alcohol and shake to dissolve the oils. Add the distilled water and shake again. Label with contents and date. Ready to use immediately.

Shake before each use. Spray 3 to 4 times into the centre of the room from about 1 metre up. Allow the mist to disperse before sitting in the sprayed area. Do not spray onto polished wood, delicate fabric, or electronics. Keep away from naked flames.
Store away from heat and sunlight. Keeps 12 months. Sweet orange evaporates faster than patchouli; after 6 to 9 months the spray may smell primarily of patchouli. Shake before every use.
